title: CommandEnvelope
description: Superseded by heroes-reforged/lockstep-envelope.schema.json for the M5 multiplayer module per docs/implementation-plans/26-replay-tampering-and-simulation-cheating-plan.md. Historical wire envelope for every multiplayer-lockstep command on the `commands` DataChannel. The HMAC tag is the first 16 bytes of HMAC-SHA256 over canonical-JSON of `[schemaVersion, seq, playerId, turn, command]`, keyed by the per-session key derived from `RTCDtlsTransport.exportKeyingMaterial('hr-cmd-mac', 32)` (or the host-minted fallback per `docs/architecture/command-stream-integrity.md` § 2a). Authoritative doctrine: `docs/architecture/command-stream-integrity.md`. Wire-format owning task: `tasks/phase-3/01-multiplayer/03-input-only-lockstep-command-serialization-plus-sequencing.md`.

seq.description: Per-(peerId, sessionId) monotonic uint32. Receivers maintain a 1024-entry ring of accepted seqs; duplicates with mismatched mac escalate to TRUST_VIOLATION_DETECTED.

playerId.description: 1-based player id; MUST match the channel's pinned peerId mapping. Cross-peer playerId values are rejected before the HMAC check runs.

turn.description: Current GameState turn at command mint.

command.description: Inner deterministic command shape; validated against `command.schema.json` after the HMAC check passes.

mac.description: Truncated HMAC-SHA256 (first 16 raw bytes / 22 url-safe base64 chars without padding) over the canonical-JSON-encoded `[schemaVersion, seq, playerId, turn, command]` array, keyed by the per-session HMAC key. A single mac mismatch dispatches TRUST_VIOLATION_DETECTED { kind: 'commandMacInvalid' } and aborts the session.
